inputs.name: num_kv_splits_ptr
inputs.meaning: Pointer to the tensor that will store the computed number of key-value splits for each sequence group.
inputs.shape: Tensor of shape (num_group * num_seq), where num_group is computed based on heads and num_seq is the number of sequences.
inputs.inference_role: Stores the computed number of key-value splits for each sequence group, used to optimize attention computation.

inputs.name: seq_lens_ptr
inputs.meaning: Pointer to the tensor containing the lengths of each sequence.
inputs.shape: Tensor of shape (num_seq), where num_seq is the number of sequences.
inputs.inference_role: Provides necessary sequence length information to calculate key-value split sizes.

inputs.name: num_seq
inputs.meaning: The number of sequences being processed.
inputs.inference_role: Used to determine how many sequences are available for processing in the model.

inputs.name: num_group
inputs.meaning: The number of groups of heads used in the model.
inputs.inference_role: Determines how heads are grouped for parallel processing in multi-head attention.

inputs.name: num_head
inputs.inference_role: Indicates the dimensionality of the attention mechanism.

inputs.name: num_kv_head
inputs.meaning: Number of heads allocated for key-value processing.
inputs.inference_role: Defines the configuration of heads specifically used for initiating key/value attention.

inputs.name: max_kv_splits
inputs.meaning: Maximum number of splits allowed for key-value pairs.
inputs.inference_role: Constrains the maximum segmentation of sequences for key-value pairs for efficient processing.

inputs.name: device_core_count
inputs.inference_role: Used to determine how to distribute computation across available device resources.

inputs.name: MAX_NUM_SEQ
inputs.meaning: Maximum number of sequences to handle for computations.
inputs.inference_role: Provides a ceiling limit for the sequences processed in a single kernel invocation.

outputs.name: num_kv_splits
outputs.meaning: The computed number of key-value splits for each group of sequences.
outputs.shape: Tensor of shape (num_group * num_seq), where each entry corresponds to a sequence group.
outputs.inference_role: Used for determining how to optimize attention calculations for the model, specifically how key-value pairs are partitioned in attention mechanisms.
